question_text: In a parallel circuit with a battery and three identical bulbs, one bulb is removed from its socket. What happens to the current flowing through the other two bulbs?

options.label: A
options.text: The current through each remaining bulb increases

options.label: B
options.text: The current through each remaining bulb decreases

options.label: C
options.text: The current through each remaining bulb stays the same

options.label: D
options.text: All the current flows to just one of the remaining bulbs

explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch is independent and receives the full voltage from the battery. Removing one bulb does not affect the voltage or current through the other branches, so the remaining bulbs continue to receive the same current.
